title: Sum with for...of
description: Given an array of numbers, return the sum using a for...of loop instead of a traditional for loop.
whatShouldHappen: Initialize a total variable to 0.
Use for...of to iterate directly over values (no index needed).
Add each value to the total.
Return the total.
patternExplanation: for...of iterates over the values of any iterable (arrays, strings, Sets, Maps) — cleaner than a for loop when you only need values, not indices.

title: Count Key Occurrences
description: Given an object, return the total number of own enumerable keys using a for...in loop.
whatShouldHappen: Initialize a counter to 0.
Use for...in to iterate over the object's keys.
Increment the counter for each own key (use hasOwnProperty).
Return the counter.
patternExplanation: for...in iterates over enumerable string keys of an object — always pair it with hasOwnProperty to avoid counting inherited prototype keys.

title: Collect Characters with for...of
description: Given a string, use a for...of loop to return an array of its individual characters.
whatShouldHappen: Create an empty result array.
Use for...of to iterate over each character of the string.
Push each character into the result array.
Return the result array.
patternExplanation: Strings are iterable in JavaScript — for...of gives you each Unicode character directly, handling multi-byte emoji/symbols correctly unlike a regular index loop.

title: Object to Pairs Array
description: Given an object, return an array of [key, value] pairs sorted by key alphabetically. Use a for...in loop.
whatShouldHappen: Create an empty result array.
Use for...in to iterate over the keys.
For each key, push [key, obj[key]] into the result array.
Sort the result array by the key (index 0 of each pair) before returning.
patternExplanation: for...in collects keys in insertion order — when you need a guaranteed order (alphabetical, numeric), sort the resulting array after collecting.

title: Flatten with for...of
description: Given an array of arrays (one level deep), return a single flat array containing all elements. Use a for...of loop. Do not use Array.prototype.flat().
whatShouldHappen: Create an empty result array.
Use an outer for...of to iterate over each sub-array.
Use an inner for...of to iterate over each element of the sub-array.
Push each element into the result.
Return the result.
patternExplanation: Nesting for...of loops is ideal for traversing collections of iterables — the outer loop visits each sub-array and the inner loop drains it into the result.
